---
title: "AI 4 Coding"
subtitle: "Day 1: Course Overview"
date: "April 20, 2026"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [simple, custom.scss]
    include-after-body:
      - text: |
          <script>
          (function() {
            function toggleFooter() {
              var f = document.querySelector(".reveal > .footer");
              if (!f || typeof Reveal === "undefined") return;
              var i = Reveal.getIndices();
              f.style.display = (i.h === 0 && i.v === 0) ? "none" : "block";
            }
            if (typeof Reveal !== "undefined") {
              Reveal.on("slidechanged", toggleFooter);
              Reveal.on("ready", toggleFooter);
              if (Reveal.isReady()) toggleFooter();
            }
          })();
          </script>
    code-copy: false
    margin: 0.10
    html-math-method: mathjax
    slide-number: c/t
    background-transition: zoom
    preload-iframes: true
    scrollable: true
    transition: fade
    preview-links: auto
    logo: /assets/img/WBG-Prosperity Horizontal-Black.svg
    footer: '[worldbank.github.io/ai4coding](https://worldbank.github.io/ai4coding) © 2026 World Bank.'
    resources:
        - /assets/img/WBG-Prosperity Horizontal-Black.svg
---


## Motivation

::::::::: {.small}
:::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Chat-Based AI tools** [Claude](https://www.anthropic.com/claude) \| [ChatGPT](https://chatgpt.com/) \| [Gemini](https://search.google/ai-in-search/) \| [MAI](https://ai.worldbank.org/chat/mai)

- Use familiar technologies: 🌐 web browser, 💬 chat, 📝 Stata editor, [**📋 copy-paste**]{.warning}.
- Improve our workflow.

[**Is this the most effective way to use AI in coding❓**]{.fg}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
There are many [**Integrated** Development Environments (IDEs)]{.highlight} for data science with AI as a part of your coding workflow.
Introducing [**Positron**]{.bg} with [**GitHub Copilot**]{.fg} is the goal of this course.
:::
::::::

[See also: previous seminar on [AI-Assisted Coding in Stata and R](https://worldbank.github.io/geoPovLearn/seminars/seminar-2026-02/overview.html) on [GeoPovlearn](https://worldbank.github.io/geoPovLearn).]{.center .alert}
:::::::::

## Course Overview {.center .small-slide}

[**Goal:** Equip economists with AI-skills for coding.]{.alert}

[2 × 3-hour sessions]{.pill} [Self-study in between]{.pill} [GitHub Copilot · Positron IDE]{.pill} [Stata & modern stat languages]{.pill}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="32%" .obj-card .card-understand}
#### Understand

- Core LLM concepts: tokens, prompts, context windows, agents
- How these affect AI-assisted coding
- AI's strengths and limitations
:::

::: {.column width="2%" }
:::

::: {.column width="33%" .obj-card .card-apply}
#### Practice

- Use AI to write code and reuse old code
- Plan execution and write prompts for multi-step tasks
- Supervise and course-correct AI-generated code
- Leverage out-of-the-box tech: `#tools`, `skills`, `/agents`, `/prompts`, etc.
:::

::: {.column width="2%" }
:::

::: {.column width="31%" .obj-card .card-control}
#### Take Control

- Adopt decent workflows for AI-assisted coding.
- Comply with data-safeguard practices.
- Use AI responsibly for coding.
:::

::::


## Course content {.small-slide}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="30%" .phase-card .phase-day1}
#### [Day 1](/day1/index.qmd#materials)
**Basics**

- Software setup & walk-through
- Key concepts: IDE, assistants, context, tokens, LLM, and how it works
- Examples: data preparation and cleaning, and security techniques
- Common techniques: managing AI's context and securing your data
:::

::: {.column width="5%"}
:::

::: {.column width="30%" .phase-card .phase-selfstudy}
#### Self-Study
**The most important part**

- Practice AI use with your own code
- [Structured examples](/selfstudy/index.qmd) and <!-- REVIEW: empty href — add target URL for learning materials --> [learning materials]() to guide you
- Examples:
  - [Testing your setup](/selfstudy/example-0.qmd){target="_blank"}
  - [Explore positron (self-study)](/selfstudy/example-1.qmd){target="_blank"}
  - [Understanding data with AI (live)](/selfstudy/example-2.qmd){target="_blank"}
  - [Reproduce, Revise, and Reuse existing analysis](/selfstudy/example-3.qmd){target="_blank"}
- Come back with questions & observations
:::

::: {.column width="5%"}
:::

::: {.column width="30%" .phase-card .phase-day2}
#### Day 2
**Go deeper**

- Debrief self-study experiences
- Examples: `planning`, using `#tools`, `skills`, `/agents`, and `/prompt-files`.
- Other relevant techniques
:::

::::

## Logistics

| | Morning | Evening (repeat) |
|-----------| ---------------------- | ----------------------|
| **Format** | In person + virtual | Virtual only |
| **Day 1** | Mon Apr 20, 9 AM–12 PM ET | Mon Apr 20, 9 PM–12 AM ET |
| **Day 2** | Thu Apr 23, 9 AM–12 PM ET | Thu Apr 23, 9 PM–12 AM ET |

- Evening repeat session is for those who cannot attend the morning session. It will cover the same material, but it will be virtual only.

- Questions during the session: **use the Teams chat** or raise your hand.

## Today's Agenda {.tiny-slide}

| Time        | Topic |
|---------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
| 9:00-9:10   | 1. Welcome and course overview (slides) |
| 9:10-9:40   | 2. Setup overview (slides). Example 0 (live) and Example 1 (self-study) |
| 9:40-10:20  | 3. Example 2: Understanding data with AI (live) |
| 10:20-10:30 | ☕ Break (10 min) |
| 10:30-10:50 | 4. Core AI-related concepts |
| 10:50-11:00 | 5. Common workflows and data safeguarding (slides) |
| 11:00-11:30 | 6. Example 3: Reproduce, Revise, and Reuse existing analysis (live/self-study) |

## Course resources {.small-slide .slide-resources}

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="40%"}
[worldbank.github.io/ai4coding](https://worldbank.github.io/ai4coding)

- References, detailed explanations, guided self-study exercises and recordings, along with in-class materials.

[github.com/worldbank/ai4coding-examples](https://github.com/worldbank/ai4coding-examples)

- Code for exercises and examples.
- Also available on OneDrive for WB staff: [ai4coding-examples](https://worldbankgroup-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/g/personal/ebukin_worldbank_org/IgCXQVakSxEpT7VJFmKp_qtnAXx8RX9iemnwfGwITkEA3bE?e=U08qTX)

[ai4coding-data](https://worldbankgroup-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/g/personal/ebukin_worldbank_org/IgBy2yGjpLJPRbM67FJpBFVgAbMRYhEPmS30H2RJSBFO_gE?e=GlDEiR) - data for exercises (OneDrive - WB only)

Previous seminar on [AI-Assisted Coding in Stata and R](https://worldbank.github.io/geoPovLearn/seminars/seminar-2026-02/overview.html) on [GeoPovlearn](https://worldbank.github.io/geoPovLearn)

:::
::: {.column width="60%"}
:::
::::

<iframe src="/index.html" class="absolute" style="right: 0; top: 0; width: 60%; height: 100%; border: 1px solid #ccc; zoom: 0.4;">

</iframe>

## What next?

1. [Set up the software](/prerequisites/software.qmd){target="_blank"}.

2. [Set up your course working folder](/day1/index.qmd#setup-overview){target="_blank"} and copy relevant materials into it.

3. Follow along with the materials for [Day 1](/day1/index.qmd).
